birdsarentreal

utils.decorators

Authentication and authorization decorators for Flask route protection.

ROLES maps human-readable role names to the integer values stored in the database and session, matching the CHECK constraint in users.sql.

Attributes

ROLES

Functions

login_required(f)

Decorator that rejects unauthenticated requests.

role_required(*roles)

Decorator factory that restricts a route to one or more named roles.

Module Contents

utils.decorators.ROLES[source]
utils.decorators.login_required(f)[source]

Decorator that rejects unauthenticated requests.

Redirects to the login page with a flash message if ‘user_id’ is not present in the session.

Parameters:

f (callable) – The route function to protect.

Returns:

The wrapped function.

Return type:

callable

utils.decorators.role_required(*roles)[source]

Decorator factory that restricts a route to one or more named roles.

Accepts any number of role name strings (e.g., ‘system_admin’, ‘grad_secretary’). Unauthenticated requests are redirected to login; authenticated requests from disallowed roles receive a 403.

Parameters:

*roles (str) – One or more keys from the ROLES dict.

Returns:

A decorator that wraps the target route function.

Return type:

callable
